steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank sits inside the participial phrase 'studying connections between the physical location in which a social media post was ______ [and] the post's content.' Two noun phrases are joined by 'and': (1) 'the physical location in which a social media post was [created]' and (2) 'the post's content.' No comma is needed when 'and' joins exactly two items inside a phrase.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — created, and the post's content: The comma before 'and' is wrong when only two items are being coordinated inside a phrase — no serial comma applies here. ✗
  • Choice B — created and the post's content: No comma before 'and' correctly joins the two noun phrases without introducing an unwanted pause or implying a third item. ✓
  • Choice C — created, and the post's content,: Adds two commas — one before 'and' (wrong) and one after 'content' that cuts the relative clause off mid-thought. ✗
  • Choice D — created and the post's content,: The trailing comma after 'content' incorrectly separates the object of 'between' from the rest of the participial phrase. ✗
That matches Choice B — created and the post's content.
